text:
If you like sunshine, low humidity, and a lake that isn't trying to slap you around, this is your weekend. Today's a lock: sunny, high right around 83, dewpoints in the comfortable 50s, and the lake lying down at 1 foot or less under a light south wind. Water's a swimmable 70, so if you're headed to the waterfront, go for it, any time today works and there's no real cutoff to plan around.

Saturday warms up nicely into the upper 80s with sunshine sticking around, still comfortable on the humidity front. The models are tight on this one, so I'm confident in it. Winds pick up just slightly into the 5 to 15 knot range by Saturday night, still nothing that should keep a paddleboard off the water. Air quality's good both days, no smoke or ozone concerns. Enjoy it, because the forecasters are already flagging unsettled weather moving in next week, and Sunday itself brings a bit more cloud cover with a stray shower possible, so this is the window to get outside.
